source: Disk-space vectors and shared common prototype distilled into a pre-KMP readiness manifest
description: Pins the disk-space model behavior families that must remain covered before disk-space conversion delegates to shared KMP code.

expected.commonDecision: Disk-space model migration may proceed only after every vector named by this readiness manifest is executable from shared commonTest, Android and iOS disk-space tests continue to reference the same vectors, byte-total and free-byte calculations remain covered, zero-fragment counts remain explicit, fragment size uses the unsigned 32-bit policy instead of inheriting Android signed-int exposure or Swift UInt32 behavior accidentally, malformed truncated varints map to typed parse errors, and the shared tests are compile-verified.

notes: This readiness manifest is a migration gate for disk-space model behavior-family completeness only; public platform adapters may preserve legacy signed or unsigned scalar exposure only after the shared unsigned policy is compile-verified.
